preamble: The phonetic transformation from a voiced aspirated bilabial plosive (bʱ) to a voiceless labiodental fricative (f) is a well-documented historical sound change, notably observed in the development of Proto-Indo-European into Latin. This shift involves a series of articulatory changes, resulting in a sound that is less obstructed and produced without vocal cord vibration.
phoneticEffects: This transformation encompasses deaspiration (loss of aspiration), devoicing (change from voiced to voiceless), fricativization or spirantization (change from a plosive to a fricative), and a slight place of articulation shift from bilabial to labiodental

examples.note: Proto-Indo-European root meaning 'to be/become' to Latin 'I was'
Proto-Indo-European root meaning 'to be/become' to Latin 'future' (participle)
Proto-Indo-European root meaning 'to carry' to Latin 'I carry'
